events.title: The Decemberists
events.description: The beloved Portland folk-rock ensemble returns to the intimate Iron Horse for two sold-out nights of storytelling and song.

events.title: Amherst Cinema: 'The Brutalist'
events.description: Brady Corbet's epic 3.5-hour drama follows Hungarian-Jewish architect László Tóth as he rebuilds his life in post-WWII America. Winner of 3 Academy Awards.

events.title: Open Mic Night
events.description: All performers welcome. Sign-ups start at 6:30. Three songs or 10 minutes max per act. Full bar.

events.title: Pioneer Valley Symphony: Spring Concert
events.description: The Pioneer Valley Symphony Orchestra performs Beethoven's Seventh Symphony alongside a world premiere from composer Caroline Shaw.

events.title: UMass Lecture: Food Sovereignty in a Changing Climate
events.description: Dr. Ayana Elizabeth Johnson discusses the intersection of climate science and food justice in small farming communities. Free and open to the public.

events.title: Comedy Night at Hawks & Reed
events.description: Three nationally touring comedians. Hosted by local favorite Marcus Webb. 21+ event, two-drink minimum.

events.title: Kids Science Saturday: Forces & Motion
events.description: Hands-on experiments for ages 5–12. Build a catapult, race cars on ramps, and explore the physics of everyday objects. Included with museum admission.

events.title: Northampton Earth Day Fair
events.description: Local vendors, live music, sustainability workshops, kids activities, and native plant sale. Hosted by Climate Action Now and the City of Northampton.

events.title: UMass MFA Thesis Exhibition
events.address: Herter Hall, 161 Presidents Dr, Amherst, MA 01003
events.description: Opening reception for the 2026 MFA Thesis Exhibition featuring work from 14 graduating artists across painting, sculpture, photography, and new media.

events.title: Drag Brunch
events.description: An all-ages celebration of community and performance art featuring local and regional drag artists, bottomless brunch, and live DJs.

events.title: Amherst Spring Farmers Market — Opening Day
events.description: The Amherst Farmers Market kicks off its 2026 season with 40+ vendors, live music, and spring produce from local farms.

events.title: Ani DiFranco
events.description: Singer-songwriter and founder of Righteous Babe Records brings her solo acoustic show to the Iron Horse. Support from local artist Alisa Amador.

events.title: Hampshire College Mainstage: 'Eurydice'
events.description: Sarah Ruhl's lyrical retelling of the Orpheus myth, directed by Hampshire students with original music. Pay-what-you-can at the door.

events.title: Trail Running Club — Weekly Run
events.description: Weekly group trail run through the Holyoke Range. 8–12 miles, mixed pace groups. Beginners welcome. Bring water and layers.

events.title: Film: 'Anora'
events.description: Sean Baker's Palme d'Or-winning film follows a young sex worker who impulsively marries the son of a Russian oligarch. Followed by audience Q&A.

events.title: Book Sale — Forbes Library
events.description: Annual spring used book sale with thousands of fiction and nonfiction titles, records, and DVDs. Proceeds support Forbes Library programs.

events.title: Smith College: Botanic Garden Plant Sale
events.description: Annual spring sale featuring native perennials, unusual annuals, tropicals, and vegetable starts grown in Smith's greenhouses. Open to the public.

events.title: Pearl Street Comedy Showcase
events.description: Monthly comedy showcase featuring five rising comedians from Boston and New York. Doors at 8:30.

events.title: Jazz Night at The Drake
events.description: The Paul Viens Quartet plays standards and originals in the intimate Drake lounge. No cover, full dinner menu.

events.title: Yoga in the Park
events.description: Free outdoor yoga every Sunday through October. All levels welcome. Bring your own mat. Donation-based; proceeds benefit Safe Passage.

events.title: Roz Chast: An Evening with the Cartoonist
events.description: New Yorker cartoonist and author Roz Chast reads from her graphic memoirs and discusses her life drawing the absurdity of everyday existence.

events.title: Easthampton Earth Day Celebration
events.description: Music, art, local vendors, kids activities, and sustainability exhibits celebrating Earth Day in the mill district. Free admission.

events.title: Northampton Spring Arts Walk
events.description: Over 40 galleries, studios, and shops open their doors for the annual spring Arts Walk. Live music, refreshments, and free admission everywhere.

events.title: Valley Gives — 24-Hour Giving Day
events.description: The annual 24-hour giving event supporting hundreds of local nonprofits across Hampshire and Hampden Counties. Give online or at hosted events.

events.title: Pioneer Valley Pride Parade & Festival
events.description: The annual Pride parade through downtown Northampton followed by a festival in Pulaski Park with live music, food vendors, and community organizations.
